description_long: This is a small, ornate pagoda structure featuring two distinct, stacked roof tiers with gracefully upturned eaves, characteristic of East Asian architecture. The roofs are finished in a blue-gray color with gold trim, and a prominent golden finial crowns the top. The lower section of the pagoda is supported by four posts and features red walls with decorative black-and-white checkered panels. The entrance is marked by a red frame, and the structure sits on a brown platform base. The interior appears open and accessible, with visible steps leading up to the main chamber. The overall design suggests a ceremonial or decorative function, suitable for placement in parks, gardens, or urban plazas.
description: A small, two-tiered East Asian pagoda with blue-gray upturned roofs, gold trim, red walls, and a decorative golden finial, set on a brown platform.
description_view_0: Front view showing the layered roofs, gold finial, and the entrance steps leading up to the main chamber.
description_view_1: Exploded side view revealing the internal structure, roof layers, and separated wall and platform components.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the checkered wall panels, stacked roofs, and the overall symmetry of the structure.
description_view_3: Exploded rear-side view showing the open interior, separated roof and wall elements, and the checkered panel detail.
